q: Environmental problems related to energy include air pollution, climate change, and water pollution.
Global warming refers to the gradual increase in the average temperature of the earth's surface.
Main greenhouse gases include carbon dioxide, methane, and chlorofluorocarbons.
CFCs help maintain the earth's protective ozone layer.
Acid rains are caused mainly by the release of sulphur dioxide ($SO_2$) and oxides of nitrogen ($NO_x$).
The oil industry adds lead to petrol to help engines run more smoothly.
Leaded fumes from vehicles increase intelligence in children.
Oil spills can damage the ecosystem and its habitats.
Excavated areas for coal mining can cause water pollution due to surface water runoff.
Photovoltaic systems make no noise and cause no pollution while in operation.
Modern wind turbine designs locate the blades downwind to reduce noise.
Infrasound from wind turbines is usually beneath the threshold of human hearing.
Wind turbines may create shadow flicker on nearby residences.
Negative impacts of dams include breeding grounds for mosquitoes and spreading of disease.
Geothermal power is considered a relatively benign source of energy.
Biomass power plants raise similar concerns about air emissions as fossil fuel plants.
In fossil fuel energy transfer, chemical energy is first converted into electrical energy.
Nuclear energy transfer involves converting nuclear energy to thermal energy, then kinetic, then electrical.
Wind energy transfer goes from kinetic energy directly to electrical energy.
Hydroelectricity energy transfer begins with gravitational potential energy.
